target.name: Tekever
target.founder: Ricardo Mendes, Vitor Cristina, Pedro Sinogas

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $631M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Percepto
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Tekever is a Series C-stage autonomous systems company in the drones and robotics sector that has raised $631M to date. The comparable universe in Drones & Autonomous consists of six private companies including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air, with no public market comparables available.

Given data constraints, a single triangulation method applies here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $631M total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C progression to the next financing stage, yielding an implied valuation of $1.1B. A peer-median approach is not feasible; while five same-sector peers exist, none meet the narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier), and a broader all-sector median would lack specificity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $912M to $1.2B, with a median point of $1.1B. This range reflects standard step-up dynamics for a company at Tekever's stage and capital intensity. The primary uncertainty is the absence of sufficiently comparable private transactions; a tighter band would require either additional same-stage peer financings or visibility into recent M&A or funding multiples within the autonomous systems cohort. The reliance on stage-progression methodology alone warrants conservative application of this range pending new market data.
